title: Yosys assert while making https://github.com/ZipCPU/wb2axip
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.33 (git sha1 2584903a060)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

I installed OSS CAD Suite, then cloned https://github.com/ZipCPU/wb2axip

$ make


### Expected Behavior

Either fail with an error, or succeed.

### Actual Behavior

```
Generating RTLIL representation for module `$paramod$2721230d014e2e2a1923a0fbb62a9fbea2305c2d\axilxbar'.
Warning: Replacing memory \r_rdata with list of registers. See axilxbar.v:1430, axilxbar.v:1425
Warning: Replacing memory \r_rresp with list of registers. See axilxbar.v:1429, axilxbar.v:1424
Warning: Replacing memory \r_bresp with list of registers. See axilxbar.v:1310, axilxbar.v:1307
Warning: Replacing memory \rgrant with list of registers. See axilxbar.v:903, axilxbar.v:898
Warning: Replacing memory \wgrant with list of registers. See axilxbar.v:755, axilxbar.v:750
Warning: Replacing memory \rrequest with list of registers. See axilxbar.v:517
Warning: Replacing memory \wrequest with list of registers. See axilxbar.v:447
Warning: Replacing memory \rrequested with list of registers. See axilxbar.v:623, axilxbar.v:601
Warning: Replacing memory \wrequested with list of registers. See axilxbar.v:590, axilxbar.v:572
Warning: Replacing memory \m_axi_rresp with list of registers. See axilxbar.v:325
Warning: Replacing memory \m_axi_rdata with list of registers. See axilxbar.v:324
Warning: Replacing memory \m_axi_bresp with list of registers. See axilxbar.v:322
Note: Assuming pure combinatorial block at axilxbar.v:774.3-780.6 in
compliance with IEC 62142(E):2005 / IEEE Std. 1364.1(E):2002. Recommending
use of @* instead of @(...) for better match of synthesis and simulation.
Note: Assuming pure combinatorial block at axilxbar.v:922.3-928.6 in
compliance with IEC 62142(E):2005 / IEEE Std. 1364.1(E):2002. Recommending
use of @* instead of @(...) for better match of synthesis and simulation.
ERROR: Assert `modules_.count(module->name) == 0' failed in kernel/rtlil.cc:922.
```

I tried to debug yosys a bit and see that this $paramod$ ast->str is used in two calls to ast.cc process_module() and the second time causes the failure in RTLIL::Design::add().

The first time process_module() is called it is from AST::process(), it is then called again from AST_INTERNAL::process_and_replace_module() and since the ast->str is the same, the design ends up with two modules with the same name.

I'm not sure what the correct behaviour would be, but two options seem to be either delete the first module as the new one would replace it, or to fetch the first module and update it with the new ast.
